<a name="decodeUncompressedStrings"></a>
## func [decodeUncompressedStrings](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L1494-L1498>)

```go
func decodeUncompressedStrings(data []byte, tupleCount uint64, blockManager *BlockManager) ([]byte, error)
```

decodeUncompressedStrings decodes DuckDB's uncompressed string segment format. DuckDB uses a heap\-based format for uncompressed strings: \- 4 bytes: total heap size \- 4 bytes: segment size \(header \+ index \+ heap\) \- 4 bytes per string: cumulative end offset in heap \(negative = overflow\) \- Heap data: concatenated string bytes \(and overflow pointers for overflow strings\)

For overflow strings \(\> \~4KB\), the offset is stored as a negative value \(e.g., \-length\). The actual string data is in a separate overflow block, and the heap contains an 8\-byte pointer \(4\-byte block\_id \+ 4\-byte offset\_in\_block\) at the beginning of the heap for each overflow string.

For single\-string segments \(e.g., 1 row\), the format is different: \- 8\-byte header \- string\_t entry \(4\-byte length \+ 12\-byte inline data\)

<a name="int64ToBytes"></a>
## func [int64ToBytes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_bitpacking.go#L399>)

```go
func int64ToBytes(values []int64, targetSize int) []byte
```

int64ToBytes converts a slice of int64 values to a byte slice with target size. The target size determines how values are truncated/stored.

Parameters:

- values: The int64 values to convert
- targetSize: Target byte size per value \(1, 2, 4, or 8\)

Returns:

- \[\]byte: Byte slice with values encoded at targetSize bytes each

<a name="packBits"></a>
## func [packBits](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_write.go#L940>)

```go
func packBits(values []uint64, bitWidth uint8) []byte
```

packBits packs the given values using the specified bit width. Bits are packed in little\-endian order \(LSB first within each byte\).

For example, packing \[0, 1, 2, 3\] with bitWidth=2: \- Value 0: bits 00 \- Value 1: bits 01 \- Value 2: bits 10 \- Value 3: bits 11 Packed as: 0b11100100 = 0xE4 \(reading LSB first\)

```go
func popcount64(x uint64) uint64
```

popcount64 counts the number of set bits in a uint64. This is the Hamming weight algorithm.

<a name="readNumericValueUnion"></a>
## func [readNumericValueUnion](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/metadata_reader.go#L2986>)

```go
func readNumericValueUnion(reader *MetadataReader) ([]byte, error)
```

readNumericValueUnion reads a NumericValueUnion nested structure for numeric types. For string types, this function is also called but the format is different: \- String format: length\-prefixed raw bytes \(varint length \+ bytes\)

Numeric format: \- Field 100: has\_value \(bool\) \- Field 101: value \(varint for integers\) \- Terminator 0xFFFF

This function handles both cases by checking if the first field looks like a valid field ID \(100/101\) or raw data.

<a name="readSegmentStateInto"></a>
## func [readSegmentStateInto](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/metadata_reader.go#L3207>)

```go
func readSegmentStateInto(reader *MetadataReader, state *ColumnSegmentState) error
```

readSegmentStateInto reads segment state from a nested structure. SegmentState format in BinarySerializer: \- Field 100: has\_validity\_mask \(bool\) \- Field 101: validity\_block\_id \(if has\_validity\_mask\) \- Field 102: validity\_offset \(if has\_validity\_mask\) \- Field 103: state\_data\_len \+ state\_data bytes \- Terminator 0xFFFF

<a name="readStatValueBytes"></a>
## func [readStatValueBytes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/metadata_reader.go#L3166>)

```go
func readStatValueBytes(reader *MetadataReader) ([]byte, error)
```

readStatValueBytes reads raw value bytes from statistics. For numeric types, this is typically a fixed\-size value \(4 or 8 bytes\). The value is stored as raw bytes, not as a varint.

<a name="readStatisticsInto"></a>
## func [readStatisticsInto](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/metadata_reader.go#L2860>)

```go
func readStatisticsInto(reader *MetadataReader, stats *BaseStatistics) error
```

readStatisticsInto reads statistics from a nested structure into BaseStatistics. Statistics format in BinarySerializer: \- Field 100: has\_stats \(bool\) \- Field 101: has\_null \(bool\) \- Field 102: stats\_type \(varint \- type discriminator, not nested\) \- Field 103: type\_specific\_stats \(nested \- contains min/max for numeric types\)

- Field 200: min\_value \(nested NumericValueUnion\)
- Field 100: has\_value \(bool\)
- Field 101: value \(varint\)
- Field 201: max\_value \(nested NumericValueUnion\)
- Field 100: has\_value \(bool\)
- Field 101: value \(varint\)

\- Terminator 0xFFFF

For CONSTANT compression, the type\_specific\_stats contains the constant value as min \(and max\).

<a name="readTypeSpecificStats"></a>
## func [readTypeSpecificStats](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/metadata_reader.go#L2930>)

```go
func readTypeSpecificStats(reader *MetadataReader) ([]byte, error)
```

readTypeSpecificStats reads a type\_specific\_stats nested structure \(Field 103 in statistics\). This structure contains: \- Field 200: min\_value \(nested NumericValueUnion\)

- Field 100: has\_value \(bool\)
- Field 101: value \(varint\)

\- Field 201: max\_value \(nested NumericValueUnion\)

- Field 100: has\_value \(bool\)
- Field 101: value \(varint\)

\- Terminator 0xFFFF

For CONSTANT compression, min and max are the same \(the constant value\).

<a name="readTypeStats"></a>
## func [readTypeStats](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/metadata_reader.go#L3104>)

```go
func readTypeStats(reader *MetadataReader) ([]byte, error)
```

readTypeStats reads a type\_stats nested structure and returns the raw min/max bytes. This is the legacy format \- newer DuckDB versions use readTypeSpecificStats instead. NumericStats format in DuckDB BinarySerializer: \- Field 100: has\_min \(bool\) \- Field 101: min\_value \(raw value, typically as varint for integers\) \- Field 102: has\_max \(bool\) \- Field 103: max\_value \(raw value\) \- Terminator 0xFFFF

<a name="requiredBitWidth"></a>
## func [requiredBitWidth](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_select.go#L293>)

```go
func requiredBitWidth(data []any) uint8
```

requiredBitWidth calculates the minimum number of bits needed to represent the range of integer values in the data.

For unsigned integers, this is the number of bits needed to represent the maximum value. For signed integers, this accounts for both positive and negative values.

Parameters:

- data: The data values as \[\]any \(expected to be integers\)

Returns the minimum bit width \(0\-64\).

<a name="serializeDatabaseHeader"></a>
## func [serializeDatabaseHeader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/header.go#L397>)

```go
func serializeDatabaseHeader(header *DatabaseHeader) []byte
```

serializeDatabaseHeader serializes a DatabaseHeader to bytes. This is the data that gets checksummed \(56 bytes\).

<a name="serializeTableDataPointer"></a>
## func [serializeTableDataPointer](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_serializer.go#L589-L593>)

```go
func serializeTableDataPointer(s *BinarySerializer, tablePointer MetaBlockPointer, totalRows uint64)
```

serializeTableDataPointer writes the table data pointer fields \(101\-103\) for a table entry. These fields are written after CreateTableInfo by DuckDB's TableDataWriter::FinalizeTable.

Format:

```
Property 101: table_pointer (MetaBlockPointer object)
  - Property 100: block_pointer (uint64, default: 0, but INVALID_INDEX is not default!)
  - Property 101: offset (uint32, default: 0)
  - MESSAGE_TERMINATOR (0xFFFF)
Property 102: total_rows (uint64)
Property 103: index_pointers (empty list of BlockPointer for forward compatibility)
```

Parameters:

- tablePointer: The MetaBlockPointer to the table storage metadata block. For empty tables \(no row groups\), use InvalidBlockID.
- totalRows: The total number of rows in the table \(sum of all row group tuple counts\). For empty tables, use 0.

```go
func skipDependenciesProperty(r *BinaryReader) error
```



<a name="skipForeignKeyProperty"></a>
## func [skipForeignKeyProperty](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_deserialize.go#L153>)

```go
func skipForeignKeyProperty(r *BinaryReader) error
```

<a name="skipNestedOrVarint"></a>
## func [skipNestedOrVarint](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/metadata_reader.go#L3077>)

```go
func skipNestedOrVarint(reader *MetadataReader) error
```

skipNestedOrVarint attempts to skip a nested object or a simple varint. This is used when we encounter an unknown field and need to skip its value.

Heuristic for deciding between nested vs varint: \- Nested structures typically start with Field 100 \(common pattern in DuckDB serialization\) \- Simple values are varints that don't look like valid field IDs in typical ranges

We check if the next bytes look like Field 100 \(0x64 0x00\), which is the most common starting field for nested structures. If so, we skip the nested structure. Otherwise, we read a simple varint.

<a name="skipNestedStructure"></a>
## func [skipNestedStructure](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/metadata_reader.go#L2792>)

```go
func skipNestedStructure(reader *MetadataReader) error
```

skipNestedStructure skips a nested BinarySerializer structure by reading until terminator. This function handles the BinarySerializer format where objects are terminated by 0xFFFF. Some fields may contain nested objects or lists which also need to be skipped.

```go
func unpack16bit(data []byte, count uint64) []uint64
```

<a name="BaseStatistics"></a>
## type [BaseStatistics](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup.go#L114-L130>)

BaseStatistics contains per\-segment statistics for a column.

```go
type BaseStatistics struct {
    // HasStats indicates whether statistics are available.
    HasStats bool

    // HasNull indicates whether NULL values exist in this segment.
    HasNull bool

    // NullCount is the count of NULL values (if tracked).
    NullCount uint64

    // DistinctCount is the approximate count of distinct values.
    DistinctCount uint64

    // StatData contains type-specific statistics stored as bytes.
    // Use NumericStatistics or StringStatistics to interpret.
    StatData []byte
}
```

<a name="BinaryDeserializer"></a>
## type [BinaryDeserializer](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_deserializer.go#L24-L27>)

BinaryDeserializer reads data written by BinarySerializer. This format uses: \- Field IDs \(uint16 little\-endian\) before each property \- Variable\-length encoding for integers \- Message terminator \(0xFFFF\) at object end

```go
type BinaryDeserializer struct {
    r   *bufio.Reader
    err error
}
```

<a name="BinaryDeserializer.Skip"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryDeserializer\) [Skip](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_deserializer.go#L342>)

```go
func (d *BinaryDeserializer) Skip(n int) error
```

Skip skips n bytes from the input stream.

<a name="BinaryDeserializer.SkipProperty"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryDeserializer\) [SkipProperty](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_deserializer.go#L328>)

```go
func (d *BinaryDeserializer) SkipProperty()
```

SkipProperty skips over a property value for an unknown property. This attempts to skip common types but may fail for complex nested structures. If the property type is unknown, this will likely corrupt the stream.

<a name="BinaryDeserializer.readBytes"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryDeserializer\) [readBytes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_deserializer.go#L47>)

```go
func (d *BinaryDeserializer) readBytes(n int) []byte
```

readBytes reads exactly n bytes.

<a name="BinaryReader"></a>
## type [BinaryReader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L18-L28>)

BinaryReader provides little\-endian binary reading utilities. It wraps an io.Reader and tracks any errors encountered during reads.

```go
type BinaryReader struct {
    // r is the underlying reader.
    r   io.Reader

    // err stores the first error encountered during reading.
    // Subsequent reads are no-ops if an error has occurred.
    err error

    // bytesRead tracks the total bytes read.
    bytesRead int64
}
```

<a name="NewBinaryReader"></a>
### func [NewBinaryReader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L31>)

```go
func NewBinaryReader(r io.Reader) *BinaryReader
```

NewBinaryReader creates a new BinaryReader wrapping the given reader.

<a name="BinaryReader.BytesRead"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [BytesRead](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L41>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) BytesRead() int64
```

BytesRead returns the total number of bytes read.

<a name="BinaryReader.Err"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [Err](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L36>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) Err() error
```

Err returns the first error that occurred during reading.

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadBool"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadBool](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L190>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadBool() bool
```

ReadBool reads a boolean value \(1 byte, 0 = false, non\-zero = true\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadBytes"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadBytes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L195>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadBytes(n int) []byte
```

ReadBytes reads exactly n bytes into a new slice.

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadFloat32"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadFloat32](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L146>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadFloat32() float32
```

ReadFloat32 reads a 32\-bit float \(little\-endian IEEE 754\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadFloat64"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadFloat64](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L168>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadFloat64() float64
```

ReadFloat64 reads a 64\-bit float \(little\-endian IEEE 754\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadInt16"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadInt16](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L91>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadInt16() int16
```

ReadInt16 reads a signed 16\-bit integer \(little\-endian\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadInt32"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadInt32](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L116>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadInt32() int32
```

ReadInt32 reads a signed 32\-bit integer \(little\-endian\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadInt64"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadInt64](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L141>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadInt64() int64
```

ReadInt64 reads a signed 64\-bit integer \(little\-endian\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadInt8"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadInt8](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L66>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadInt8() int8
```

ReadInt8 reads a single signed byte.

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadString"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadString](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L215>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadString() string
```

ReadString reads a length\-prefixed string \(uint32 length followed by bytes\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadUint16"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadUint16](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L71>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadUint16() uint16
```

ReadUint16 reads an unsigned 16\-bit integer \(little\-endian\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadUint32"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadUint32](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L96>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadUint32() uint32
```

ReadUint32 reads an unsigned 32\-bit integer \(little\-endian\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadUint64"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadUint64](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L121>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadUint64() uint64
```

ReadUint64 reads an unsigned 64\-bit integer \(little\-endian\).

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadUint8"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadUint8](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L46>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadUint8() uint8
```

ReadUint8 reads a single unsigned byte.

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadUvarint"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadUvarint](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L235>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadUvarint() uint64
```

ReadUvarint reads a variable\-length unsigned integer.

<a name="BinaryReader.ReadVarint"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [ReadVarint](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L263>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) ReadVarint() int64
```

ReadVarint reads a variable\-length signed integer \(zigzag encoded\).

<a name="BinaryReader.Remaining"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [Remaining](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L297>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) Remaining() int64
```

Remaining returns the number of bytes remaining to be read. This only works if the underlying reader implements io.Seeker. Returns \-1 if remaining cannot be determined.

<a name="BinaryReader.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [Reset](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L332>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) Reset()
```

Reset clears the error state, allowing reads to continue. Use with caution \- this should only be used after handling an error.

<a name="BinaryReader.Skip"></a>
### func \(\*BinaryReader\) [Skip](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_reader.go#L270>)

```go
func (br *BinaryReader) Skip(n int)
```

Skip skips n bytes in the reader.

<a name="BinarySerializer"></a>
## type [BinarySerializer](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L17-L21>)

BinarySerializer writes data in DuckDB's BinarySerializer format. This format uses: \- Field IDs \(uint16 little\-endian\) before each property \- Variable\-length encoding for integers \- Message terminator \(0xFFFF\) at object end

```go
type BinarySerializer struct {
    w         io.Writer
    err       error
    debugMode bool // optional, for debugging
}
```

<a name="NewBinarySerializer"></a>
### func [NewBinarySerializer](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L24>)

```go
func NewBinarySerializer(w io.Writer) *BinarySerializer
```

NewBinarySerializer creates a new BinarySerializer that writes to w.

<a name="BinarySerializer.Err"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [Err](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L32>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) Err() error
```

Err returns the first error encountered during serialization. Once an error occurs, all subsequent write operations are no\-ops.

<a name="BinarySerializer.OnListBegin"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [OnListBegin](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L76>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) OnListBegin(count uint64)
```

OnListBegin writes the list count as a varint.

<a name="BinarySerializer.OnObjectBegin"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [OnObjectBegin](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L59>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) OnObjectBegin()
```

OnObjectBegin is called when starting to serialize an object. Objects don't have headers in BinarySerializer, so this is a no\-op.

<a name="BinarySerializer.OnObjectEnd"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [OnObjectEnd](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L65>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) OnObjectEnd()
```

OnObjectEnd is called when finishing object serialization. Writes MESSAGE\_TERMINATOR\_FIELD\_ID \(0xFFFF\) as little\-endian uint16.

<a name="BinarySerializer.OnPropertyBegin"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [OnPropertyBegin](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L47>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) OnPropertyBegin(fieldID uint16, tag string)
```

OnPropertyBegin writes a field ID as little\-endian uint16. The tag parameter is for debugging only and is not written to output.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteBool"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteBool](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L84>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteBool(value bool)
```

WriteBool writes a boolean value as a single byte \(0 or 1\).

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteBytes"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteBytes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L178>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteBytes(value []byte)
```

WriteBytes writes a byte slice with its length as a varint followed by the bytes.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteInt16"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteInt16](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L137>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteInt16(value int16)
```

WriteInt16 writes a signed 16\-bit integer using ZigZag encoding then varint.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteInt32"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteInt32](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L146>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteInt32(value int32)
```

WriteInt32 writes a signed 32\-bit integer using ZigZag encoding then varint.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteInt64"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteInt64](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L155>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteInt64(value int64)
```

WriteInt64 writes a signed 64\-bit integer using ZigZag encoding then varint.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteInt8"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteInt8](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L128>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteInt8(value int8)
```

WriteInt8 writes a signed 8\-bit integer using ZigZag encoding then varint.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteList"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteList](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L290-L295>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteList(fieldID uint16, tag string, count uint64, fn func(idx uint64))
```

WriteList writes a list of items. The callback fn is called for each item index. Automatically handles OnPropertyBegin, OnListBegin with count, and fn\(i\) for each item.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteObject"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteObject](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L269>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteObject(fieldID uint16, tag string, fn func())
```

WriteObject writes a nested object. The callback fn writes the object's properties. Automatically handles OnPropertyBegin, OnObjectBegin, fn\(\), and OnObjectEnd.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteObjectList"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteObjectList](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L316-L321>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteObjectList(fieldID uint16, tag string, count uint64, fn func(idx uint64))
```

WriteObjectList writes a list of objects. Each item is wrapped in object begin/end.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteProperty"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteProperty](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L201>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteProperty(fieldID uint16, tag string, value interface{})
```

WriteProperty writes a property with field ID and value. Supports: bool, uint8, uint16, uint32, uint64, int8, int16, int32, int64, string

<a name="BinarySerializer.WritePropertyWithDefault"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WritePropertyWithDefault](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L239-L243>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WritePropertyWithDefault(fieldID uint16, tag string, value, defaultValue interface{})
```

WritePropertyWithDefault writes a property only if value differs from defaultValue. Uses reflect.DeepEqual to compare values.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteRawBytes"></a>


WriteRawBytes writes bytes directly without a length prefix.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteString"></a>


WriteString writes a string with its length as a varint followed by the bytes.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteUint16"></a>

```go

```

WriteUint16 writes an unsigned 16\-bit integer as a varint.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteUint32"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteUint32](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L112>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteUint32(value uint32)
```

WriteUint32 writes an unsigned 32\-bit integer as a varint.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteUint64"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteUint64](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L120>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteUint64(value uint64)
```

WriteUint64 writes an unsigned 64\-bit integer as a varint.

<a name="BinarySerializer.WriteUint8"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [WriteUint8](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L96>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) WriteUint8(value uint8)
```

WriteUint8 writes an unsigned 8\-bit integer as a varint.

<a name="BinarySerializer.writeBytes"></a>
### func \(\*BinarySerializer\) [writeBytes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_serializer.go#L38>)

```go
func (s *BinarySerializer) writeBytes(data []byte)
```

writeBytes writes raw bytes to the underlying writer. If an error has already occurred, this is a no\-op.

<a name="BinaryWriter"></a>
## type [BinaryWriter](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_writer.go#L11-L21>)

BinaryWriter provides little\-endian binary writing utilities. It wraps an io.Writer and tracks any errors encountered during writes.

```go
type BinaryWriter struct {
    // w is the underlying writer.
    w   io.Writer

    // err stores the first error encountered during writing.
    // Subsequent writes are no-ops if an error has occurred.
    err error

    // bytesWritten tracks the total bytes written.
    bytesWritten int64
}
```

<a name="NewBinaryWriter"></a>
### func [NewBinaryWriter](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/binary_writer.go#L24>)

```go

```

```go
func (bw *BinaryWriter) BytesWritten() int64
```

BytesWritten returns the total number of bytes written.

```go
func (bw *BinaryWriter) Err() error
```

Err returns the first error that occurred during writing.

```go
func (bw *BinaryWriter) Reset()
```

Reset clears the error state, allowing writes to continue. Use with caution \- this should only be used after handling an error.

```go

```

WriteBool writes a boolean value \(1 byte, 0 = false, 1 = true\).


<a name="BitpackingMode"></a>
## type [BitpackingMode](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/format.go#L152>)

BitpackingMode represents the internal mode for BITPACKING compression.

```go
type BitpackingMode uint8
```

<a name="BitpackingAuto"></a>Bitpacking mode constants.

```go
const (
    BitpackingAuto          BitpackingMode = 0 // AUTO - choose best mode
    BitpackingConstant      BitpackingMode = 1 // All values are the same
    BitpackingConstantDelta BitpackingMode = 2 // Constant difference between values
    BitpackingDeltaFOR      BitpackingMode = 3 // Delta + Frame of Reference
    BitpackingFOR           BitpackingMode = 4 // Frame of Reference only
)
```

<a name="Block"></a>
## type [Block](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/block.go#L33-L47>)

Block represents a single block in the DuckDB file. Each block starts with an 8\-byte checksum followed by data.

```go
type Block struct {
    // ID is the block number (0-indexed from data blocks start).
    ID  uint64

    // Type indicates the type of data stored in this block.
    Type BlockType

    // Checksum is stored at the BEGINNING of the block (first 8 bytes).
    // It covers the Data bytes only (not itself).
    Checksum uint64

    // Data contains the block contents after the checksum.
    // Size is BlockAllocSize - BlockChecksumSize.
    Data []byte
}
```

<a name="BlockCache"></a>
## type [BlockCache](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/block.go#L114-L130>)

BlockCache provides LRU caching for blocks to reduce disk I/O.

```go
type BlockCache struct {
    // capacity is the maximum number of blocks to cache.
    capacity int

    // blocks maps block IDs to cached blocks.
    blocks map[uint64]*list.Element

    // order is a doubly-linked list for LRU ordering.
    // Front is most recently used, back is least recently used.
    order *list.List

    // stats tracks cache performance metrics.
    stats CacheStats

    // mu protects concurrent access to the cache.
    mu  sync.RWMutex
}
```

<a name="NewBlockCache"></a>
### func [NewBlockCache](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/block.go#L163>)

```go
func NewBlockCache(capacity int) *BlockCache
```

NewBlockCache creates a new BlockCache with the given capacity.

<a name="BlockCache.Clear"></a>

```go
func (bc *BlockCache) Clear()
```

Clear removes all blocks from the cache.


Get retrieves a block from the cache. Returns nil if the block is not cached.


Invalidate removes a specific block from the cache.

Put adds a block to the cache. Evicts the least recently used block if at capacity.


<a name="BlockManager"></a>
## type [BlockManager](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/block.go#L51-L75>)

BlockManager handles reading and writing blocks to the DuckDB file. It provides caching and manages the free list for block allocation.

```go
type BlockManager struct {
    // file is the underlying file handle.
    file *os.File

    // blockSize is the size of each block in bytes (default 256KB).
    blockSize uint64

    // blockCount is the total number of allocated blocks.
    blockCount uint64

    // freeList tracks which blocks are available for allocation.
    freeList *FreeListManager

    // cache provides LRU caching of recently accessed blocks.
    cache *BlockCache

    // version is the storage format version.
    version uint64

    // closed indicates whether the block manager has been closed.
    closed bool

    // mu protects concurrent access to the block manager.
    mu  sync.RWMutex
}
```

<a name="NewBlockManager"></a>
### func [NewBlockManager](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/block.go#L139>)

```go
func NewBlockManager(file *os.File, blockSize uint64, cacheSize int) *BlockManager
```

NewBlockManager creates a new BlockManager for the given file.

```go

```

AllocateBlock allocates a new block for writing. Returns a block ID from the free list if available, otherwise allocates new. Note: Block 0 is reserved for metadata and will not be returned by this method. Use AllocateMetadataBlock\(\) to explicitly get block 0.

<a name="BlockManager.AllocateMetadataBlock"></a>

```go

```

AllocateMetadataBlock returns block 0, which is reserved for metadata. This should only be called by CatalogWriter when writing catalog data.

<a name="BlockReadAhead"></a>
## type [BlockReadAhead](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/optimize.go#L239-L244>)

BlockReadAhead provides read\-ahead hints for sequential block access.

```go
type BlockReadAhead struct {
    bm            *BlockManager
    lastBlockID   uint64
    readAheadSize int
    pending       []uint64
}
```

<a name="NewBlockReadAhead"></a>
### func [NewBlockReadAhead](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/optimize.go#L247>)

```go
func NewBlockReadAhead(bm *BlockManager, readAheadSize int) *BlockReadAhead
```

NewBlockReadAhead creates a read\-ahead manager for the block manager.

<a name="BlockReadAhead.ReadBlock"></a>
### func \(\*BlockReadAhead\) [ReadBlock](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/optimize.go#L261>)

```go
func (ra *BlockReadAhead) ReadBlock(id uint64) (*Block, error)
```

ReadBlock reads a block and triggers read\-ahead for sequential access. If the access pattern is sequential \(reading block N after N\-1\), this will pre\-fetch the next readAheadSize blocks in the background.

<a name="BlockReadAhead.prefetch"></a>
### func \(\*BlockReadAhead\) [prefetch](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/optimize.go#L273>)

```go
func (ra *BlockReadAhead) prefetch(startID uint64)
```

prefetch loads blocks into the cache in the background.

<a name="CatalogEntry"></a>
## type [CatalogEntry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog.go#L8-L17>)

CatalogEntry is the interface implemented by all catalog entry types. It provides common accessors for catalog metadata operations.

```go
type CatalogEntry interface {
    // Type returns the CatalogType identifying this entry kind.
    Type() CatalogType

    // GetName returns the name of the catalog entry.
    GetName() string

    // GetSchema returns the schema name of the catalog entry.
    GetSchema() string
}
```

<a name="DeserializeCatalogEntry"></a>
### func [DeserializeCatalogEntry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_entry_deserialize.go#L280>)

```go
func DeserializeCatalogEntry(r *BinaryReader) (CatalogEntry, error)
```

DeserializeCatalogEntry reads a catalog entry from the binary reader. It first reads the catalog type byte, then dispatches to the appropriate deserializer based on the type.

Returns the deserialized CatalogEntry and any error encountered. For unknown or invalid catalog types, returns nil and an error.

<a name="CatalogType"></a>
## type [CatalogType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/format.go#L183>)

CatalogType represents the type of catalog entry. Values match DuckDB's catalog\_entry\_type.hpp definitions.

```go
type CatalogType uint8
```

<a name="CatalogInvalid"></a>Catalog type constants.

```go
const (
    CatalogInvalid                CatalogType = 0   // INVALID
    CatalogTableEntry             CatalogType = 1   // TABLE_ENTRY
    CatalogSchemaEntry            CatalogType = 2   // SCHEMA_ENTRY
    CatalogViewEntry              CatalogType = 3   // VIEW_ENTRY
    CatalogIndexEntry             CatalogType = 4   // INDEX_ENTRY
    CatalogPreparedStatement      CatalogType = 5   // PREPARED_STATEMENT
    CatalogSequenceEntry          CatalogType = 6   // SEQUENCE_ENTRY
    CatalogCollationEntry         CatalogType = 7   // COLLATION_ENTRY
    CatalogTypeEntry              CatalogType = 8   // TYPE_ENTRY
    CatalogDatabaseEntry          CatalogType = 9   // DATABASE_ENTRY
    CatalogTableFunctionEntry     CatalogType = 25  // TABLE_FUNCTION_ENTRY
    CatalogScalarFunctionEntry    CatalogType = 26  // SCALAR_FUNCTION_ENTRY
    CatalogAggregateFunctionEntry CatalogType = 27  // AGGREGATE_FUNCTION_ENTRY
    CatalogPragmaFunctionEntry    CatalogType = 28  // PRAGMA_FUNCTION_ENTRY
    CatalogCopyFunctionEntry      CatalogType = 29  // COPY_FUNCTION_ENTRY
    CatalogMacroEntry             CatalogType = 30  // MACRO_ENTRY
    CatalogTableMacroEntry        CatalogType = 31  // TABLE_MACRO_ENTRY
    CatalogDeletedEntry           CatalogType = 51  // DELETED_ENTRY
    CatalogRenamedEntry           CatalogType = 52  // RENAMED_ENTRY
    CatalogSecretEntry            CatalogType = 71  // SECRET_ENTRY
    CatalogSecretTypeEntry        CatalogType = 72  // SECRET_TYPE_ENTRY
    CatalogSecretFunctionEntry    CatalogType = 73  // SECRET_FUNCTION_ENTRY
    CatalogDependencyEntry        CatalogType = 100 // DEPENDENCY_ENTRY
)
```

<a name="CatalogType.String"></a>
### func \(CatalogType\) [String](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/format.go#L240>)

```go
func (c CatalogType) String() string
```

String returns the string representation of a CatalogType.

<a name="CatalogWriter"></a>
## type [CatalogWriter](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L99-L133>)

CatalogWriter writes catalog metadata to DuckDB format blocks. It serializes all catalog entries \(schemas, tables, views, indexes, sequences, types\) to metadata blocks and creates a master catalog index block that the database header points to.

```go
type CatalogWriter struct {
    // blockManager handles block allocation and I/O.
    blockManager *BlockManager

    // catalog is the catalog to serialize.
    catalog *DuckDBCatalog

    // metaBlocks tracks written metadata block IDs.
    metaBlocks []uint64

    // binaryFormatBlocks tracks block IDs allocated during WriteBinaryFormat.
    // These are the metadata blocks that need to be registered in MetadataManager state.
    binaryFormatBlocks []uint64

    // tableRowGroups maps table OID to row group pointers.
    tableRowGroups map[uint64]*tableRowGroupEntry

    // closed indicates whether the writer has been closed.
    closed bool

    // duckdbCompatMode enables DuckDB CLI compatibility mode.
    // When true, metadata is only written if there are row groups.
    // This allows DuckDB CLI to open files as empty databases.
    duckdbCompatMode bool

    // useBinarySerializer enables BinarySerializer format.
    // When true, uses DuckDB-compatible BinarySerializer instead of custom format.
    useBinarySerializer bool

    // metadataManager manages metadata sub-blocks for BinarySerializer format.
    metadataManager *MetadataBlockManager

    // mu protects concurrent access.
    mu  sync.Mutex
}
```

<a name="NewCatalogWriter"></a>
### func [NewCatalogWriter](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L140>)

```go
func NewCatalogWriter(bm *BlockManager, catalog *DuckDBCatalog) *CatalogWriter
```

NewCatalogWriter creates a new CatalogWriter for serializing catalog metadata.

Parameters:

- bm: BlockManager for block allocation and I/O
- catalog: The DuckDBCatalog to serialize

<a name="CatalogWriter.AddRowGroupPointer"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [AddRowGroupPointer](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L169>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) AddRowGroupPointer(tableOID uint64, rgp *RowGroupPointer) error
```

AddRowGroupPointer registers a row group for a table. Row group pointers are stored separately from table catalog entries and are written to their own metadata blocks.

Parameters:

- tableOID: The table object ID \(index in catalog.Tables\)
- rgp: The row group pointer to register

<a name="CatalogWriter.BlockManager"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [BlockManager](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L1161>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) BlockManager() *BlockManager
```

BlockManager returns the block manager used by this writer.

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) Catalog() *DuckDBCatalog
```

Catalog returns the catalog being written.

<a name="CatalogWriter.Close"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L1075>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) Close() error
```

Close closes the catalog writer and releases resources. This does NOT flush any pending writes \- call Write\(\) first if needed.

<a name="CatalogWriter.GetBinaryFormatBlocks"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [GetBinaryFormatBlocks](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L1151>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) GetBinaryFormatBlocks() []uint64
```

GetBinaryFormatBlocks returns the block IDs allocated during WriteBinaryFormat. These blocks need to be registered in the MetadataManager state for DuckDB compatibility.

<a name="CatalogWriter.GetMetaBlocks"></a>


<a name="CatalogWriter.GetTableRowGroupCount"></a>

<a name="CatalogWriter.IsClosed"></a>

```go

```

IsClosed returns whether the writer has been closed.

<a name="CatalogWriter.MetaBlockCount"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [MetaBlockCount](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L1091>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) MetaBlockCount() int
```

MetaBlockCount returns the number of metadata blocks written.

<a name="CatalogWriter.SetDuckDBCompatMode"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [SetDuckDBCompatMode](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L155>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) SetDuckDBCompatMode(enabled bool)
```

SetDuckDBCompatMode enables or disables DuckDB CLI compatibility mode. When enabled, metadata is only written if there are row groups with actual data. This also enables BinarySerializer format for DuckDB\-compatible serialization. By default, compatibility mode is disabled.

<a name="CatalogWriter.Write"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [Write](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L212>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) Write() (MetaBlockPointer, error)
```

Write serializes the entire catalog to metadata blocks. Returns the MetaBlockPointer for the database header, which points to the catalog index block containing references to all catalog entries.

For an empty catalog \(no entries\), returns an invalid MetaBlockPointer with BlockID set to InvalidBlockID. This signals to DuckDB that there is no catalog metadata to load.

The write process:

1. Write all schemas
2. Write all tables with row group pointers
3. Write all views
4. Write all indexes
5. Write all sequences
6. Write all custom types
7. Write catalog index block pointing to all entries

<a name="CatalogWriter.WriteBinaryFormat"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [WriteBinaryFormat](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L702>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) WriteBinaryFormat() (MetaBlockPointer, error)
```

WriteBinaryFormat serializes the catalog using DuckDB's BinarySerializer format. This produces DuckDB CLI\-compatible output by writing to metadata sub\-blocks.

DuckDB's metadata sub\-block format \(16\-byte header\): \- First 8 bytes: Previous/metadata pointer \(InvalidBlockID for first block\) \- Next 8 bytes: Next sub\-block pointer \(InvalidBlockID if last\) \- Remaining bytes: Actual serialized catalog data

The MetaBlockPointer in database header encodes: BlockID \(bits 0\-55\) \+ BlockIndex \(bits 56\-63\)

The caller must hold w.mu when calling this method.

NOTE: Currently, table storage metadata \(row group pointers\) is NOT written because DuckDB expects a complex format that includes column segment metadata, statistics, HyperLogLog structures, etc. Tables with data will appear empty when opened by DuckDB CLI. Full row data compatibility is a work in progress.

<a name="CatalogWriter.collectCatalogEntries"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [collectCatalogEntries](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L1017>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) collectCatalogEntries() []CatalogEntry
```

collectCatalogEntries collects all catalog entries in the correct order. The caller must hold w.mu when calling this method.

<a name="CatalogWriter.getTableStorageSubBlockCount"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [getTableStorageSubBlockCount](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L1175-L1178>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) getTableStorageSubBlockCount(columnCount int, columns []ColumnDefinition) int
```

getTableStorageSubBlockCount returns the number of sub\-blocks needed for a table's storage metadata. This matches the logic in DuckDBStorage.getTableStorageSubBlockCount.

<a name="CatalogWriter.serializeTableStorageData"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [serializeTableStorageData](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L936>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) serializeTableStorageData(rowGroups []*RowGroupPointer) ([]byte, error)
```

serializeTableStorageData serializes table storage data \(TableStatistics \+ row groups\) for sub\-block 1. This creates the data that goes at the table\_pointer location.

Format: 1. 8\-byte next\_ptr header \(pointing to next sub\-block or InvalidBlockID\) 2. TableStatistics \(BinarySerializer format with 50 bytes padding\) 3. row\_group\_count \(raw uint64, 8 bytes\) 4. Each row group as BinarySerializer objects

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeCatalogIndex"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeCatalogIndex](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L469>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeCatalogIndex() (MetaBlockPointer, error)
```

writeCatalogIndex writes the master catalog index block. This block is what the database header MetaBlock points to. It contains references to all catalog entry blocks.

DuckDB metadata block format: \- Bytes 0\-7: Next block pointer \(InvalidBlockID if last/only block\) \- Bytes 8\+: Serialized catalog data

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeEntry"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeEntry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L294>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeEntry(entry CatalogEntry) error
```

writeEntry writes a single catalog entry to a metadata block. It serializes the entry, allocates a block, writes the data, and tracks the block ID.

DuckDB metadata block format: \- Bytes 0\-7: Next block pointer \(InvalidBlockID if last/only block\) \- Bytes 8\+: Serialized catalog entry

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeIndexEntry"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeIndexEntry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L338>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeIndexEntry(index *IndexCatalogEntry) error
```

writeIndexEntry writes an index catalog entry.

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeRowGroupPointers"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeRowGroupPointers](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L415>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeRowGroupPointers(tableOID uint64) (uint64, error)
```

writeRowGroupPointers writes all row group pointers for a table to a metadata block. Returns the block ID where row group pointers are stored, or 0 if no row groups.

DuckDB metadata block format: \- Bytes 0\-7: Next block pointer \(InvalidBlockID if last/only block\) \- Bytes 8\+: Row group pointer data

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeRowGroupPointersToBlock"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeRowGroupPointersToBlock](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L578>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeRowGroupPointersToBlock(rowGroups []*RowGroupPointer) (uint64, error)
```

writeRowGroupPointersToBlock writes row group pointers to a metadata block. Returns the block ID where row group pointers are stored.

DuckDB's table data format at the tablePointer location: 1. TableStatistics \(BinarySerializer wrapped with Begin/End\) 2. row\_group\_count \(raw uint64, 8 bytes \- NOT varint\!\) 3. For each row group: BinarySerializer wrapped RowGroupPointer

Each RowGroupPointer object has: \- Field 100: row\_start \(uint64 varint\) \- Field 101: tuple\_count \(uint64 varint\) \- Field 102: data\_pointers \(vector\<MetaBlockPointer\>\) \- Field 103: delete\_pointers \(vector\<MetaBlockPointer\>\) \- empty \- Terminator 0xFFFF

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeSchemaEntry"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeSchemaEntry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L328>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeSchemaEntry(schema *SchemaCatalogEntry) error
```

writeSchemaEntry writes a schema catalog entry.

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeSequenceEntry"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeSequenceEntry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L343>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeSequenceEntry(sequence *SequenceCatalogEntry) error
```

writeSequenceEntry writes a sequence catalog entry.

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeTableEntry"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeTableEntry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L359>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeTableEntry(table *TableCatalogEntry, tableOID uint64) error
```

writeTableEntry writes table metadata including row group pointers. Tables need special handling to include row group metadata that points to where the table data is stored.

DuckDB metadata block format: \- Bytes 0\-7: Next block pointer \(InvalidBlockID if last/only block\) \- Bytes 8\+: Serialized table entry data

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeTableStorageMetadata"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeTableStorageMetadata](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L527>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeTableStorageMetadata() (map[uint64]TableStorageInfo, error)
```

writeTableStorageMetadata computes table storage info without writing to disk yet. Returns a map of tableOID \-\> TableStorageInfo for use during catalog serialization.

The actual table storage data will be written as part of WriteBinaryFormat\(\) to ensure it's placed in sub\-blocks of block 0, which is required for DuckDB CLI compatibility.

The caller must hold w.mu when calling this method.

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeTypeEntry"></a>
### func \(\*CatalogWriter\) [writeTypeEntry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_writer.go#L348>)

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeTypeEntry(typeEntry *TypeCatalogEntry) error
```

writeTypeEntry writes a custom type catalog entry.

<a name="CatalogWriter.writeViewEntry"></a>

```go
func (w *CatalogWriter) writeViewEntry(view *ViewCatalogEntry) error
```

<a name="ColumnData"></a>
## type [ColumnData](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L30-L69>)

ColumnData represents decompressed column data from a row group segment. It contains the raw decompressed data, validity mask for NULL tracking, and metadata about the column values.

```go
type ColumnData struct {
    // Data contains the raw decompressed data bytes.
    // Values are stored in little-endian format.
    Data []byte

    // Validity tracks NULL values for this column.
    // If nil, all values are valid (no NULLs).
    Validity *ValidityMask

    // TupleCount is the number of tuples in this column segment.
    TupleCount uint64

    // TypeID is the logical type of the column.
    TypeID LogicalTypeID

    // Statistics contains per-segment statistics if available.
    Statistics *BaseStatistics

    // ChildData contains decompressed child column data for complex types.
    // For LIST types: contains the list element values
    // For STRUCT/MAP: would contain field/key-value data
    ChildData *ColumnData

    // Offsets contains offset data for LIST types.
    // Each offset indicates where each list's elements end in ChildData.
    // The offsets are cumulative, so list[i] contains elements from
    // Offsets[i-1] (or 0 for i=0) to Offsets[i].
    Offsets []uint64

    // ChildTypeID stores the logical type of child elements for LIST types.
    ChildTypeID LogicalTypeID

    // ChildrenData contains decompressed child column data for STRUCT types.
    // Each element corresponds to a struct field in the same order as StructFields.
    ChildrenData []*ColumnData

    // StructFields contains the field definitions for STRUCT types.
    // Each element contains the field name and type information.
    StructFields []StructField
}
```

<a name="decompressColumn"></a>
### func [decompressColumn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L833-L837>)

```go
func decompressColumn(blockManager *BlockManager, dp *DataPointer, typeID LogicalTypeID) (*ColumnData, error)
```

decompressColumn decompresses column data from blocks using the DataPointer.

<a name="decompressListColumn"></a>
### func [decompressListColumn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L1011-L1014>)

```go
func decompressListColumn(blockManager *BlockManager, dp *DataPointer) (*ColumnData, error)
```

decompressListColumn decompresses a LIST column. LIST columns in DuckDB are stored with: 1. Offset data \(uint64 cumulative offsets indicating list boundaries\) in the main DataPointer 2. Child element data stored either in a separate ChildPointer OR inline after the offsets 3. Validity: NULL mask for list values \(stored in ValidityPointer\)

The ChildPointer field of the DataPointer contains the child element data's DataPointer, which is recursively decompressed to get the child ColumnData.

For UNCOMPRESSED LIST columns without ChildPointer, child data is stored INLINE immediately after the offset array in the same data segment.

<a name="decompressMapColumn"></a>
### func [decompressMapColumn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L1310-L1313>)

```go
func decompressMapColumn(blockManager *BlockManager, dp *DataPointer) (*ColumnData, error)
```

decompressMapColumn decompresses a MAP column. MAP is stored as LIST\(STRUCT\(key, value\)\) in DuckDB. This means we reuse the LIST decompression but the child type is STRUCT.

<a name="decompressStructColumn"></a>
### func [decompressStructColumn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L1185-L1188>)

```go
func decompressStructColumn(blockManager *BlockManager, dp *DataPointer) (*ColumnData, error)
```

decompressStructColumn decompresses a STRUCT column. STRUCT columns in DuckDB store each field as a separate child column. The struct validity mask applies to the entire struct \(not individual fields\). Each field has its own child ColumnData with its own validity mask.

<a name="decompressStructFields"></a>
### func [decompressStructFields](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L1226-L1231>)

```go
func decompressStructFields(blockManager *BlockManager, structDP *DataPointer, fields []StructField, tupleCount uint64) ([]*ColumnData, error)
```

decompressStructFields decompresses all fields of a STRUCT column. In DuckDB, each struct field is stored as a separate child column. The STRUCT's DataPointer contains: \- ChildPointer: the first field's DataPointer \- ChildPointers: additional field DataPointers \(for fields 1, 2, ...\)

<a name="readInlineChildData"></a>
### func [readInlineChildData](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L1121-L1126>)

```go
func readInlineChildData(data []byte, childTypeID LogicalTypeID, elementCount uint64, blockManager *BlockManager) (*ColumnData, error)
```

readInlineChildData reads child element data that is stored inline after the offset array. This handles both fixed\-size types \(INTEGER, BIGINT, etc.\) and variable\-size types \(VARCHAR\).

<a name="readInlineVarcharChildren"></a>
### func [readInlineVarcharChildren](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L1154-L1158>)

```go
func readInlineVarcharChildren(data []byte, elementCount uint64, blockManager *BlockManager) (*ColumnData, error)
```

readInlineVarcharChildren reads VARCHAR child elements stored inline in a LIST column. The format is similar to uncompressed strings: heap\-based with cumulative offsets.

<a name="ColumnData.GetValue"></a>
### func \(\*ColumnData\) [GetValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L74>)

```go
func (c *ColumnData) GetValue(rowIdx uint64) (any, bool)
```

GetValue retrieves the value at the given row index. Returns \(value, isValid\) where isValid is false if the value is NULL. The returned value type depends on the column's TypeID.

<a name="ColumnData.IsNull"></a>
### func \(\*ColumnData\) [IsNull](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L293>)

```go
func (c *ColumnData) IsNull(rowIdx uint64) bool
```

IsNull returns true if the value at the given row index is NULL.

<a name="ColumnData.decodeFixedValue"></a>
### func \(\*ColumnData\) [decodeFixedValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L335>)

```go
func (c *ColumnData) decodeFixedValue(data []byte) any
```

decodeFixedValue decodes a fixed\-size value from bytes.

<a name="ColumnData.getListValue"></a>
### func \(\*ColumnData\) [getListValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L111>)

```go
func (c *ColumnData) getListValue(rowIdx uint64) (any, bool)
```

getListValue retrieves a LIST value at the given row index. Returns the list elements as \[\]interface\{\}.

<a name="ColumnData.getMapValue"></a>
### func \(\*ColumnData\) [getMapValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L203>)

```go
func (c *ColumnData) getMapValue(rowIdx uint64) (any, bool)
```

getMapValue retrieves a MAP value at the given row index. Returns the map as map\[K\]V where K is typically string and V is the value type. DuckDB stores MAP as LIST\(STRUCT\(key, value\)\) internally.

<a name="ColumnData.getStructValue"></a>
### func \(\*ColumnData\) [getStructValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L166>)

```go
func (c *ColumnData) getStructValue(rowIdx uint64) (any, bool)
```

getStructValue retrieves a STRUCT value at the given row index. Returns the struct fields as map\[string\]interface\{\}.

<a name="ColumnData.getVariableSizeValue"></a>
### func \(\*ColumnData\) [getVariableSizeValue](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L302>)

```go
func (c *ColumnData) getVariableSizeValue(rowIdx uint64) (any, bool)
```

getVariableSizeValue retrieves a variable\-size value \(VARCHAR, BLOB\). Variable\-size values are stored with length prefix.

<a name="ColumnDefinition"></a>
## type [ColumnDefinition](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog.go#L167-L194>)

ColumnDefinition represents a column definition in a table.

```go
type ColumnDefinition struct {
    // Name is the column name.
    Name string

    // Type is the logical type ID of the column.
    Type LogicalTypeID

    // TypeModifiers contains type-specific information (precision, scale, child types, etc.).
    TypeModifiers TypeModifiers

    // Nullable indicates whether the column allows NULL values.
    Nullable bool

    // HasDefault indicates whether a default value is defined.
    HasDefault bool

    // DefaultValue is the default value expression (as SQL string).
    DefaultValue string

    // Generated indicates if this is a generated column.
    Generated bool

    // GeneratedExpression is the expression for generated columns.
    GeneratedExpression string

    // CompressionType is the compression hint for this column.
    CompressionType CompressionType
}
```

<a name="ColumnSegmentState"></a>
## type [ColumnSegmentState](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup.go#L90-L111>)

ColumnSegmentState contains segment metadata including validity mask info.

```go
type ColumnSegmentState struct {
    // HasValidityMask indicates whether there are NULLs in this segment.
    HasValidityMask bool

    // ValidityBlock points to where the validity mask is stored (if separate).
    // If HasValidityMask is true and ValidityBlock is invalid, the validity
    // mask is inlined in StateData.
    ValidityBlock BlockPointer

    // ValidityCompression is the compression type for the validity column.
    // Relevant when HasValidityMask is true and validity is stored separately.
    ValidityCompression CompressionType

    // ValidityHasNull indicates whether the validity column's statistics
    // report HasNull=true (meaning all values in this column are NULL).
    // This is used when validity uses CONSTANT compression with block ID 127.
    ValidityHasNull bool

    // StateData contains additional compression-specific state.
    // For segments with inlined validity masks, this contains the mask data.
    StateData []byte
}
```

<a name="NewColumnSegmentState"></a>
### func [NewColumnSegmentState](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup.go#L868>)

```go
func NewColumnSegmentState(hasValidityMask bool, validityBlock BlockPointer) *ColumnSegmentState
```

NewColumnSegmentState creates a new ColumnSegmentState.

```go
func (s *ColumnSegmentState) Deserialize(r *BinaryReader) error
```

<a name="ComparisonReport"></a>
## type [ComparisonReport](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/debug_compare.go#L37-L61>)

ComparisonReport contains the results of comparing two DuckDB files.

```go
type ComparisonReport struct {
    // File1Path is the path to the first file.
    File1Path string

    // File2Path is the path to the second file.
    File2Path string

    // Differences is a list of all byte-level differences.
    Differences []DifferenceLocation

    // FirstDifference is the offset of the first difference, or -1 if files are identical.
    FirstDifference int64

    // TotalDifferences is the total number of different bytes.
    TotalDifferences int64

    // FilesIdentical is true if the files are byte-for-byte identical.
    FilesIdentical bool

    // File1Size is the size of file 1 in bytes.
    File1Size int64

    // File2Size is the size of file 2 in bytes.
    File2Size int64
}
```

<a name="CompareDBFiles"></a>
### func [CompareDBFiles](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/debug_compare.go#L150>)

```go
func CompareDBFiles(path1, path2 string) (*ComparisonReport, error)
```

CompareDBFiles compares two DuckDB files byte\-by\-byte. Returns a detailed comparison report.

```go
func (r *ComparisonReport) String() string
```

String returns a formatted summary of the comparison report.

<a name="CompressionEstimate"></a>
## type [CompressionEstimate](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_select.go#L783-L792>)

CompressionEstimate provides an estimate of the compressed size for each compression algorithm, helping choose the best compression.

```go
type CompressionEstimate struct {
    // Algorithm is the compression type.
    Algorithm CompressionType

    // EstimatedSize is the estimated compressed size in bytes.
    EstimatedSize int

    // CompressionRatio is the ratio of original size to compressed size.
    CompressionRatio float64
}
```

<a name="EstimateCompressionSizes"></a>
### func [EstimateCompressionSizes](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_select.go#L804-L808>)

```go
func EstimateCompressionSizes(data []any, typeID LogicalTypeID, valueSize int) []CompressionEstimate
```

EstimateCompressionSizes estimates the compressed size for different compression algorithms. This can be used for more accurate compression selection when compression ratio is more important than speed.

Parameters:

- data: The data values as \[\]any
- typeID: The logical type of the data
- valueSize: The size in bytes of each value

Returns a slice of CompressionEstimate sorted by estimated size \(smallest first\).

<a name="CompressionHeader"></a>
## type [CompressionHeader](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_write.go#L45-L54>)

CompressionHeader contains metadata about a compressed segment. This may be used as a prefix to compressed data or stored separately in the row group metadata.

```go
type CompressionHeader struct {
    // Type is the compression algorithm used.
    Type CompressionType

    // ValueSize is the byte size of each uncompressed value.
    ValueSize uint32

    // Count is the number of values in the segment.
    Count uint64
}
```

<a name="CompressionStats"></a>
## type [CompressionStats](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_select.go#L13-L48>)

CompressionStats holds statistics about a data segment used for selecting the optimal compression algorithm.

```go
type CompressionStats struct {
    // TotalCount is the total number of values in the data.
    TotalCount int

    // UniqueCount is the number of unique (distinct) values.
    UniqueCount int

    // NullCount is the number of NULL values.
    NullCount int

    // RunCount is the number of consecutive runs of identical values.
    // A run is a sequence of one or more identical adjacent values.
    RunCount int

    // MinValue is the minimum non-NULL value (for numeric types).
    MinValue any

    // MaxValue is the maximum non-NULL value (for numeric types).
    MaxValue any

    // BitWidth is the minimum number of bits needed to represent
    // the range of integer values (for integer types).
    BitWidth uint8

    // IsMonotonic indicates whether the values are monotonically
    // increasing or decreasing (for numeric types).
    IsMonotonic bool

    // ConstantDelta indicates whether all consecutive differences
    // are the same (for delta encoding).
    ConstantDelta bool

    // AvgDelta is the average delta between consecutive values
    // (for numeric types).
    AvgDelta float64
}
```

<a name="AnalyzeData"></a>
### func [AnalyzeData](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_select.go#L124>)

```go
func AnalyzeData(data []any, typeID LogicalTypeID) *CompressionStats
```

AnalyzeData computes statistics about the data for compression selection. It analyzes the data to determine characteristics like uniqueness, runs, value ranges, and monotonicity.

Parameters:

- data: The data values as \[\]any
- typeID: The logical type of the data

Returns a CompressionStats struct with the analysis results.

<a name="CompressionType"></a>
## type [CompressionType](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/format.go#L70>)

CompressionType represents the compression algorithm used for column data. Values match DuckDB's compression\_type.hpp definitions.

```go
type CompressionType uint8
```

<a name="CompressionAuto"></a>Compression type constants matching DuckDB's compression algorithms.

```go
const (
    CompressionAuto         CompressionType = 0  // AUTO - automatic selection
    CompressionUncompressed CompressionType = 1  // UNCOMPRESSED - raw data
    CompressionConstant     CompressionType = 2  // CONSTANT - single value repeated
    CompressionRLE          CompressionType = 3  // RLE - run-length encoding
    CompressionDictionary   CompressionType = 4  // DICTIONARY - dictionary compression
    CompressionPFORDelta    CompressionType = 5  // PFOR_DELTA - packed FOR with delta
    CompressionBitPacking   CompressionType = 6  // BITPACKING - bit-packed integers
    CompressionFSST         CompressionType = 7  // FSST - fast static symbol table
    CompressionCHIMP        CompressionType = 8  // CHIMP - time series float compression
    CompressionPATAS        CompressionType = 9  // PATAS - time series compression
    CompressionALP          CompressionType = 10 // ALP - adaptive lossless float
    CompressionALPRD        CompressionType = 11 // ALPRD - ALP with real doubles
    CompressionZSTD         CompressionType = 12 // ZSTD - Zstandard compression
    CompressionRoaring      CompressionType = 13 // ROARING - roaring bitmap
    CompressionEmpty        CompressionType = 14 // EMPTY - internal empty marker
    CompressionDictFSST     CompressionType = 15 // DICT_FSST - dictionary + FSST
)
```

<a name="SelectBestCompression"></a>
### func [SelectBestCompression](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_select.go#L916>)

```go
func SelectBestCompression(data []any, typeID LogicalTypeID, valueSize int) CompressionType
```

SelectBestCompression analyzes data and selects the compression algorithm with the best estimated compression ratio. This is more thorough than SelectCompression but requires more computation.

Parameters:

- data: The data values as \[\]any
- typeID: The logical type of the data
- valueSize: The size in bytes of each value

Returns the CompressionType with the best estimated compression ratio.

<a name="SelectCompression"></a>
### func [SelectCompression](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_select.go#L67>)

```go
func SelectCompression(data []any, typeID LogicalTypeID) CompressionType
```

SelectCompression analyzes data and selects the best compression algorithm. It uses heuristics based on data characteristics to choose an algorithm that balances compression ratio and encoding/decoding speed.

The selection logic follows these priorities:

1. CONSTANT: If all values are identical
2. RLE: If there are few runs relative to total count \(\< 30%\)
3. DICTIONARY: If there are few unique values relative to total count \(\< 50%\)
4. PFOR\_DELTA: For monotonic integer sequences
5. BITPACKING: For integers with small bit width relative to full width \(\< 70%\)
6. UNCOMPRESSED: Default fallback

Parameters:

- data: The data values as \[\]any
- typeID: The logical type of the data

Returns the recommended CompressionType for the data.

<a name="SelectCompressionForIntegers"></a>
### func [SelectCompressionForIntegers](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_select.go#L470>)

```go
func SelectCompressionForIntegers(data []int64) CompressionType
```

SelectCompressionForIntegers applies integer\-specific heuristics for compression selection. Integer types can benefit from bit\-packing and delta encoding in addition to the general compression algorithms.

Parameters:

- data: The integer values as \[\]int64

Returns the recommended CompressionType for the integer data.

<a name="SelectCompressionForStrings"></a>
### func [SelectCompressionForStrings](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_select.go#L424>)

```go
func SelectCompressionForStrings(data []string) CompressionType
```

SelectCompressionForStrings applies string\-specific heuristics for compression selection. Strings have different characteristics than numeric types, so specialized heuristics can improve compression efficiency.

Parameters:

- data: The string values

Returns the recommended CompressionType for the string data.

<a name="TryCompress"></a>
### func [TryCompress](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_write.go#L218-L222>)

```go
func TryCompress(compression CompressionType, data []byte, valueSize int) ([]byte, CompressionType, error)
```

TryCompress attempts to compress data using the specified algorithm, falling back to uncompressed if the specified algorithm fails or is not suitable for the data.

Parameters:

- compression: The preferred compression algorithm
- data: The raw data bytes to compress
- valueSize: Size in bytes of each value

Returns:

- \[\]byte: The compressed data
- CompressionType: The actual compression type used
- error: nil on success \(may use different compression than requested\)

<a name="CompressionType.String"></a>
### func \(CompressionType\) [String](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/format.go#L113>)

```go
func (c CompressionType) String() string
```

String returns the string representation of a CompressionType.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleCompressionType\_String demonstrates compression type string representation.

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/dukdb/dukdb-go/internal/storage/duckdb"
)

func main() {
	compressions := []duckdb.CompressionType{
		duckdb.CompressionUncompressed,
		duckdb.CompressionConstant,
		duckdb.CompressionRLE,
		duckdb.CompressionDictionary,
		duckdb.CompressionBitPacking,
		duckdb.CompressionPFORDelta,
	}

	for _, c := range compressions {
		fmt.Printf("%d: %s\n", c, c.String())
	}
}
```

#### Output

```
1: UNCOMPRESSED
2: CONSTANT
3: RLE
4: DICTIONARY
6: BITPACKING
5: PFOR_DELTA
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Compressor"></a>
## type [Compressor](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_write.go#L30-L40>)

Compressor is the interface for all compression algorithms. Implementations compress raw data bytes into a compressed format that can later be decompressed using the corresponding Decompressor.

```go
type Compressor interface {
    // Compress compresses the input data.
    // Parameters:
    //   - data: The raw data bytes to compress
    //   - valueSize: Size in bytes of each value
    // Returns the compressed data or an error if compression fails.
    Compress(data []byte, valueSize int) ([]byte, error)

    // Type returns the compression type identifier for this compressor.
    Type() CompressionType
}
```

<a name="GetCompressor"></a>
### func [GetCompressor](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/compress_write.go#L282>)

```go
func GetCompressor(compression CompressionType) Compressor
```

GetCompressor returns the appropriate Compressor for the given compression type. This allows for object\-oriented usage patterns where a single compressor instance can be reused for multiple compressions.

Returns nil if the compression type is not supported.

<a name="Config"></a>
## type [Config](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/storage.go#L48-L60>)

Config contains configuration options for DuckDBStorage.

```go
type Config struct {
    // ReadOnly opens the file in read-only mode.
    ReadOnly bool

    // BlockCacheSize is the number of blocks to cache (default: 128).
    BlockCacheSize int

    // CreateIfNotExists creates the file if it doesn't exist.
    CreateIfNotExists bool

    // VectorSize is the number of rows per vector (default: 2048).
    VectorSize uint64
}
```

<a name="DefaultConfig"></a>

```go

```

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleDefaultConfig demonstrates the default configuration options.

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/dukdb/dukdb-go/internal/storage/duckdb"
)

func main() {
	config := duckdb.DefaultConfig()

	fmt.Printf("ReadOnly: %v\n", config.ReadOnly)
	fmt.Printf("BlockCacheSize: %d\n", config.BlockCacheSize)
	fmt.Printf("CreateIfNotExists: %v\n", config.CreateIfNotExists)
	fmt.Printf("VectorSize: %d\n", config.VectorSize)
}
```

#### Output

```
ReadOnly: false
BlockCacheSize: 128
CreateIfNotExists: true
VectorSize: 2048
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Constraint"></a>
## type [Constraint](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog.go#L352-L370>)

Constraint represents a table constraint.

```go
type Constraint struct {
    // Type is the constraint type.
    Type ConstraintType

    // Name is the constraint name (optional).
    Name string

    // ColumnIndices is the list of column indices involved in the constraint.
    // For PRIMARY KEY, UNIQUE: the columns forming the key.
    // For NOT NULL: single column index.
    // For CHECK: empty (expression references columns by name).
    ColumnIndices []uint64

    // Expression is the check constraint expression (for CHECK constraints).
    Expression string

    // ForeignKey contains foreign key specific information.
    ForeignKey *ForeignKeyInfo
}
```
