<a name="TableScanner"></a>
## type [TableScanner](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L57-L93>)

TableScanner scans a table across multiple row groups with projection pushdown. It supports both row\-by\-row iteration and batch scanning of entire row groups.

```go
type TableScanner struct {
    // blockManager is used to read blocks from the file.
    blockManager *BlockManager

    // tableEntry contains table metadata (columns, types).
    tableEntry *TableCatalogEntry

    // rowGroups contains pointers to all row groups for the table.
    rowGroups []*RowGroupPointer

    // projection contains column indices to read (nil = all columns).
    projection []int

    // columnTypes contains the logical type of each table column.
    columnTypes []LogicalTypeID

    // columnModifiers contains type modifiers for each column (for complex types).
    columnModifiers []TypeModifiers

    // currentRGIdx is the index of the current row group being scanned.
    currentRGIdx int

    // currentReader is the reader for the current row group.
    currentReader *RowGroupReader

    // currentRowIdx is the current row index within the current row group.
    currentRowIdx uint64

    // totalRows is the total number of rows scanned so far.
    totalRows uint64

    // closed indicates whether the scanner has been closed.
    closed bool

    // mu protects concurrent access to the scanner.
    mu  sync.Mutex
}
```

<a name="NewTableScanner"></a>
### func [NewTableScanner](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L97-L101>)

```go
func NewTableScanner(bm *BlockManager, table *TableCatalogEntry, rowGroups []*RowGroupPointer) *TableScanner
```

NewTableScanner creates a new TableScanner for reading a table across multiple row groups. The scanner supports projection pushdown \- only specified columns are read from disk.

<a name="TableScanner.Advance"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [Advance](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L285>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) Advance()
```

Advance moves to the next row after reading current values. This should be called after processing the current row with GetRow/GetColumn.

<a name="TableScanner.Close"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [Close](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L320>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) Close()
```

Close releases resources held by the scanner. After calling Close, the scanner cannot be used.

<a name="TableScanner.CurrentRowGroupIndex"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [CurrentRowGroupIndex](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L417>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) CurrentRowGroupIndex() int
```

CurrentRowGroupIndex returns the index of the current row group being scanned. Returns \-1 if no row group is currently active.

<a name="TableScanner.GetColumn"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [GetColumn](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L244>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) GetColumn(colIdx int) (any, error)
```

GetColumn returns a specific column value from the current row. The colIdx is an index into the projection array, not the table column index.

<a name="TableScanner.GetRow"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [GetRow](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L203>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) GetRow() ([]any, error)
```

GetRow returns the current row's values for projected columns. Returns an error if no current row exists.

<a name="TableScanner.Next"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [Next](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L173>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) Next() bool
```

Next advances to the next row and returns true if there is a row to read. Returns false when all rows have been scanned or the scanner is closed.

<a name="TableScanner.Projection"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [Projection](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L157>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) Projection() []int
```

Projection returns the current projection \(nil if reading all columns\).

<a name="TableScanner.Reset"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [Reset](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L299>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) Reset()
```

Reset resets the scanner to the beginning. After reset, call Next\(\) to move to the first row.

<a name="TableScanner.RowGroupCount"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [RowGroupCount](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L409>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) RowGroupCount() int
```

RowGroupCount returns the total number of row groups in the table.

<a name="TableScanner.ScanAll"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [ScanAll](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L376>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) ScanAll() ([]*ScanResult, error)
```

ScanAll scans all row groups and returns combined results. Results are returned in row group order.

<a name="TableScanner.ScanRowGroup"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [ScanRowGroup](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L341>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) ScanRowGroup(rgIdx int) (*ScanResult, error)
```

ScanRowGroup scans an entire row group by index and returns batch results. This is more efficient than row\-by\-row iteration for bulk operations.

<a name="TableScanner.SetProjection"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [SetProjection](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L127>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) SetProjection(columnIdxs []int) error
```

SetProjection sets which columns to read. Pass nil to read all columns. Column indices must be valid for the table schema.

<a name="TableScanner.TotalRows"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [TotalRows](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L402>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) TotalRows() uint64
```

TotalRows returns the total number of rows scanned so far.

<a name="TableScanner.TotalTableRows"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [TotalTableRows](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L424>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) TotalTableRows() uint64
```

TotalTableRows returns the total number of rows across all row groups.

<a name="TableScanner.advanceRowGroupLocked"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [advanceRowGroupLocked](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L438>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) advanceRowGroupLocked() bool
```

advanceRowGroupLocked advances to the next row group. Must be called with lock held. Returns false if there are no more row groups.

<a name="TableScanner.getProjectedIndicesLocked"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [getProjectedIndicesLocked](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L461>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) getProjectedIndicesLocked() []int
```

getProjectedIndicesLocked returns the column indices to read. If no projection is set, returns all column indices. Must be called with lock held.

<a name="TableScanner.readProjectedColumnsLocked"></a>
### func \(\*TableScanner\) [readProjectedColumnsLocked](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L476>)

```go
func (s *TableScanner) readProjectedColumnsLocked() ([]*ColumnData, error)
```

readProjectedColumnsLocked reads the projected columns from the current row group. Must be called with lock held.

<a name="TableScannerIterator"></a>
## type [TableScannerIterator](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L487-L492>)

TableScannerIterator provides a simplified row\-by\-row interface for TableScanner. It implements an iterator pattern without requiring explicit Advance calls.

```go
type TableScannerIterator struct {
    scanner *TableScanner
    row     []any
    err     error
    hasRow  bool
}
```

<a name="NewTableScannerIterator"></a>


NewTableScannerIterator creates an iterator over the table scanner.

<a name="TableScannerIterator.Close"></a>

```go
func (it *TableScannerIterator) Close()
```

Close releases resources and closes the underlying scanner.

<a name="TableScannerIterator.Err"></a>

```go
func (it *TableScannerIterator) Err() error
```

Err returns any error that occurred during iteration.

<a name="TableScannerIterator.Next"></a>
### func \(\*TableScannerIterator\) [Next](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L503>)

```go
func (it *TableScannerIterator) Next() bool
```

Next advances to the next row and returns true if there is a row. If an error occurs during scanning, it can be retrieved via Err\(\).

<a name="TableScannerIterator.Row"></a>
### func \(\*TableScannerIterator\) [Row](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/table_scanner.go#L530>)

```go
func (it *TableScannerIterator) Row() []any
```

Row returns the current row's values. Returns nil if there is no current row.

<a name="TableStorageInfo"></a>
## type [TableStorageInfo](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog_serializer.go#L481-L489>)

TableStorageInfo contains the storage metadata for a table entry. This is used when serializing table entries to write the correct table\_pointer and total\_rows fields.

```go
type TableStorageInfo struct {
    // TablePointer points to the table storage metadata block.
    // For empty tables (no row groups), use InvalidBlockID.
    TablePointer MetaBlockPointer

    // TotalRows is the sum of all row group tuple counts.
    // For empty tables, use 0.
    TotalRows uint64
}
```

<a name="TypeModifiers"></a>
## type [TypeModifiers](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog.go#L198-L237>)

TypeModifiers contains type\-specific parameters for complex types. Not all fields are used for every type \- only the relevant fields are populated.

```go
type TypeModifiers struct {
    // Width is the precision/width for DECIMAL, CHAR, etc.
    Width uint8

    // Scale is the scale for DECIMAL type.
    Scale uint8

    // Length is the length for fixed-size types like CHAR, ARRAY.
    Length uint32

    // ChildType is the element type for LIST, ARRAY types.
    ChildType *TypeModifiers

    // ChildTypeID is the LogicalTypeID of the child type.
    ChildTypeID LogicalTypeID

    // KeyType is the key type for MAP.
    KeyType *TypeModifiers

    // KeyTypeID is the LogicalTypeID of the key type.
    KeyTypeID LogicalTypeID

    // ValueType is the value type for MAP.
    ValueType *TypeModifiers

    // ValueTypeID is the LogicalTypeID of the value type.
    ValueTypeID LogicalTypeID

    // StructFields is the list of fields for STRUCT type.
    StructFields []StructField

    // EnumValues is the list of values for ENUM type.
    EnumValues []string

    // UnionMembers is the list of members for UNION type.
    UnionMembers []UnionMember

    // Collation is the collation name for string types.
    Collation string
}
```

UUID represents a 128\-bit UUID value.

```go
type UUID [16]byte
```

<a name="UUID.String"></a>
### func \(UUID\) [String](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/type_convert.go#L1332>)

```go
func (u UUID) String() string
```

String returns the UUID in standard format \(8\-4\-4\-4\-12\).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

ExampleUUID\_String demonstrates UUID string formatting.

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"

	"github.com/dukdb/dukdb-go/internal/storage/duckdb"
)

func main() {
	// Create a UUID from bytes
	uuid := duckdb.UUID{
		0x12, 0x34, 0x56, 0x78,
		0x9a, 0xbc,
		0xde, 0xf0,
		0x12, 0x34,
		0x56, 0x78, 0x9a, 0xbc, 0xde, 0xf0,
	}

	fmt.Printf("UUID: %s\n", uuid.String())
}
```

#### Output

```
UUID: 12345678-9abc-def0-1234-56789abcdef0
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ValidityMask"></a>
## type [ValidityMask](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup.go#L164-L174>)

ValidityMask tracks NULL values for a column segment. Uses a bit array where 1 = valid \(not NULL\) and 0 = null.

```go
type ValidityMask struct {
    // data is the bit array for tracking validity.
    // Each uint64 holds validity for 64 rows.
    data []uint64

    // allValid is an optimization flag - if true, no NULLs exist.
    allValid bool

    // rowCount is the number of rows this mask covers.
    rowCount uint64
}
```

<a name="DecodeValidityMask"></a>
### func [DecodeValidityMask](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L2047>)

```go
func DecodeValidityMask(data []byte, tupleCount uint64) (*ValidityMask, error)
```

DecodeValidityMask is an alias for decodeValidityMask for external use.

<a name="DeserializeValidityMask"></a>
### func [DeserializeValidityMask](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup.go#L706>)

```go
func DeserializeValidityMask(data []byte) (*ValidityMask, error)
```

DeserializeValidityMask deserializes a ValidityMask from bytes.

<a name="NewValidityMask"></a>
### func [NewValidityMask](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup.go#L185>)

```go
func NewValidityMask(rowCount uint64) *ValidityMask
```

NewValidityMask creates a new ValidityMask for the given row count. By default, all rows are marked as valid \(not NULL\).

<a name="NewValidityMaskAllNull"></a>
### func [NewValidityMaskAllNull](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup.go#L203>)

```go
func NewValidityMaskAllNull(rowCount uint64) *ValidityMask
```

NewValidityMaskAllNull creates a new ValidityMask where all rows are NULL. All bits are initialized to 0 \(invalid/NULL\).

<a name="NewValidityMaskFromData"></a>
### func [NewValidityMaskFromData](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup.go#L215>)

```go
func NewValidityMaskFromData(data []uint64, rowCount uint64) *ValidityMask
```

NewValidityMaskFromData creates a ValidityMask from existing bit data.

<a name="decodeValidityMask"></a>
### func [decodeValidityMask](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L2025>)

```go
func decodeValidityMask(data []byte, tupleCount uint64) (*ValidityMask, error)
```

decodeValidityMask decodes a validity mask from raw bytes.

<a name="encodeColumnData"></a>
### func [encodeColumnData](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_writer.go#L372-L376>)

```go
func encodeColumnData(data []any, typeID LogicalTypeID, mods *TypeModifiers) ([]byte, *ValidityMask, error)
```

encodeColumnData converts a slice of any values to raw bytes with validity tracking. Returns the encoded bytes, validity mask, and any error.

<a name="extractEmbeddedValidityMask"></a>
### func [extractEmbeddedValidityMask](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L1859-L1864>)

```go
func extractEmbeddedValidityMask(compressedData []byte, compression CompressionType, valueSize int, tupleCount uint64) (*ValidityMask, error)
```

extractEmbeddedValidityMask extracts validity mask embedded in RLE or Bitpacking compressed data. For nullable columns using these compression types without a separate validity child, DuckDB stores the validity mask after the compression metadata.

<a name="readValidityFromPointer"></a>
### func [readValidityFromPointer](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/rowgroup_reader.go#L1938-L1942>)

```go
func readValidityFromPointer(bm *BlockManager, validityPtr *DataPointer, tupleCount uint64) (*ValidityMask, error)
```

readValidityFromPointer reads validity mask from a child ColumnData pointer. In DuckDB, nullable columns store validity as a separate nested ColumnData. The validity is stored as a bit vector where each bit indicates if a value is valid \(1\) or NULL \(0\).

<a name="readValidityMask"></a>

```go
func readValidityMask(bm *BlockManager, state ColumnSegmentState, tupleCount uint64) (*ValidityMask, error)
```

readValidityMask reads the validity mask from block or inline data.

<a name="ViewCatalogEntry"></a>
## type [ViewCatalogEntry](<https://github.com/connerohnesorge/dukdb-go/blob/main/internal/storage/duckdb/catalog.go#L472-L490>)

ViewCatalogEntry represents a view definition in the catalog.

```go
type ViewCatalogEntry struct {
    // CreateInfo contains common catalog entry fields.
    CreateInfo

    // Name is the view name.
    Name string

    // Query is the SELECT query that defines the view.
    Query string

    // Aliases is the list of column aliases (may be empty if using query column names).
    Aliases []string

    // Types is the list of column types for the view.
    Types []LogicalTypeID

    // TypeModifiers contains type-specific information for each column.
    TypeModifiers []TypeModifiers
}
```

<a name="ConvertViewToDuckDB"></a>

